phrases.intent_en: Ask how much a dozen oysters costs
phrases.cultural_note: Oyster prices vary by size and season. The market offers oysters to eat on the spot or take away.

responses.english: It depends on the size. Prices are posted there.
responses.phonetic: sah day-PAHN duh lah TAH-yuh. lay PREE son ah-fee-SHAY LAH
responses.local: Ça dépend de la taille. Les prix sont affichés là.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a dozen oysters to eat here
phrases.text_local: Une dozen d'huîtres à manger ici, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: oon DOO-zahn WEE-truh ah mahn-ZHAY ee-SEE seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: The market has stands where you can eat fresh oysters on the spot, typically with lemon and bread. They come with a small fork.

responses.english: Yes, with lemon and bread. Here you go.
responses.phonetic: wee byan soor, see say dee-spoh-nee-BL
responses.local: Oui, avec du citron et du pain. Voilà.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the different sizes of oysters
phrases.text_local: Quelles sont les différentes tailles d'huîtres ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel son lay day-fay-RANT TAH-yuh WEE-truh
phrases.cultural_note: Oysters are graded by size: No. 1 (largest, 'pied de cheval'), No. 2 ('demi'), No. 3 ('numéro 3'), and No. 4 (smallest, 'brettve').

responses.english: From one to four. Two and three are the most common.
responses.local: De un à quatre. Le deux et le trois sont les plus courants.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the oysters are fresh today
phrases.cultural_note: Oysters are best in months with 'R' (September through April). In summer, they spawn and are less flavorful. Look for the 'R' rule.

responses.phonetic: wee, el son byan fresh oh-zhoor-DWEE

phrases.intent_en: Ask about pairing wine with oysters
phrases.text_local: Quel vin thérapeut-on avec des huîtres ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel van tay-ray-peh-TOH ah-vek day WEE-truh
phrases.cultural_note: The classic pairing is a crisp Muscadet or Chablis. For something different, try a dry cidre from Brittany with your oysters.

responses.english: A nice cold Muscadet or a Breton cider.
responses.phonetic: wee byan soor, see say dee-spoh-nee-BL
responses.local: Un Muscadet bien frais ou un cidre breton.
